steps.title: Identify the performance comparison
steps.content: The passage contrasts the road-map method (described as “not very accurate”) with Hosseini's program, which “more accurately identified sidewalks, and it even distinguished between concrete and brick.” The key signal is that the program outperformed the baseline.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
Superior means higher in quality or performance—exactly what the program demonstrated.

The other three fail to capture this superiority: “changed” is directionless, “similar” implies equal performance (contradicting the accuracy difference), and “supportive” doesn't measure performance quality.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
